knowledge_gaps: DOGE-style spending reduction macro impact
The 5y5y forward rate is contaminated by 50-75bp of fiscal term premium, overstating the market's neutral rate estimate
ACM vs. Kim-Wright term premium model divergence (30-50bp) is itself a diagnostic signal of fiscal regime uncertainty
AI capex ($200-250B/yr from hyperscalers) may partially substitute for the Kalecki fiscal channel in supporting corporate profits
2Y-SPX correlation is -0.25 to -0.35 while 30Y-SPX is +0.05 to +0.15, reflecting monetary dominance at front end and fiscal supply dominance at long end
The tariff-fiscal nexus is net-negative for deficit reduction, with revenue gains offset by GDP drag
Interaction between fiscal deficits and term premium
The Fama-French HML factor has ~1-2% incremental annual return attributable to fiscal policy after controlling for cycle and monetary policy
Private credit opacity ($1.8T) represents the critical unknown Minsky variable
Fiscal contraction has an asymmetrically higher multiplier (1.2-1.8x) than fiscal expansion (0.6-0.9x) at current debt levels
Equity-implied HY spreads are 50-75bp wider than actual spreads, reflecting differential fiscal optimism between credit and equity investors
Fiscal austerity that eliminates biofuel tax credits while preserving volume mandates creates market distortions
Central bank independence erosion operates through appointment dynamics on a 4-8 year cycle, with the U.S. currently at early stage
Fiscal multipliers are overstated when food prices are elevated due to import leakage
The bilateral U.S.-China fiscal accelerator adds an estimated 0.5-1.0% GDP to combined fiscal commitments per decade
A U.S. sovereign credit downgrade below AA would trigger forced selling and collateral substitution through Basel III risk weighting, central bank repo eligibility, and insurance capital requirements
IRA rollback under DOGE would remove 800kt-1.2Mt of incremental copper demand from 2028-2032 balance sheets
The crowded duration short (CFTC 95th percentile) creates contrarian mean-reversion risk on a 3-6 month tactical horizon
The Fama-French HML factor carries ~1-2% annualized excess return attributable to fiscal policy
Under fiscal dominance, stock-bond correlation flips positive, duration becomes risk factor, and quality factor premium expands 2-3x
G7 fiscal multiplier estimates under current debt levels
